title: Crystal halberd
wikitext:
{{External|rs}} {{Otheruses|def=no|the historical version|Crystal halberd (historical)|other uses|Crystal halberd (disambiguation)}} {{Infobox Item |version1 = Active |version2 = Inactive |version3 = New |name1 = Crystal halberd |name2 = Crystal halberd (inactive) |name3 = Crystal halberd |image = [[File:Crystal halberd.png]] |release = [[25 July]] [[2019]] |update = Song of the Elves |aka = Chally |members = Yes |quest = No |tradeable = No |placeholder = Yes |equipable = Yes |stackable = No |noteable = No |options1 = Wield, Check, Revert, Drop |options2 = Wield, Revert, Drop |options3 = Wield, Check, Revert, Drop |wornoptions1 = Check |wornoptions2 = None |wornoptions3 = Check |examine = A nice sturdy pike. |value = 300000 |alchable = No |weight = 1.814 |id1 = 23987 |id2 = 23989 |id3 = 24125 |leagueRegion = Tirannwn & Kandarin }} [[File:Crystal halberd detail.png|left|120px]] The '''crystal halberd''' is a [[Degradation|degradable]] two-handed [[Weapons/Categories#Polearm|polearm]] made from a [[crystal weapon seed]]. It requires completion of the [[Hard Western Provinces Diary]] to wield, as well as 70 [[Attack]], 35 [[Strength]] and 50 [[Agility]]. In order to obtain a crystal halberd (with 2,500 charges), one must talk to [[Islwyn]] who will offer to sell a crystal halberd for 750,000 coins. Alternatively, one can take a [[crystal weapon seed]] to [[Ilfeen]] to be recharged into a crystal halberd with 2,500 charges starting at a price of 750,000 coins, lowering by 150,000 each time afterwards, eventually reaching a recharge price of 150,000 coins. With access to [[Prifddinas]] and completion of the [[Hard Western Provinces Diary]], the [[singing bowl]] can be used with 78 Crafting and Smithing and 40 [[crystal shard]]s to turn a crystal weapon seed into a crystal halberd. Players without the stat requirements can speak to [[Conwenna]] or [[Reese]] and have them create the crystal halberd for you for 20 additional crystal shards. The crystal halberd is significantly more powerful than a [[dragon halberd]], making it a good choice as a melee [[safespot]] weapon. The crystal halberd can deal full damage to the [[Corporeal Beast]], despite not being a spear, but it '''must''' be on the stab attack style. The crystal halberd is used for its special attack, which can deal high damage against large enemies or groups of enemies. However, its special attack is bested in DPS by the special attacks of both the [[dragon dagger]] and [[dragon claws]] against most monsters, making its use largely centred around last-hitting and AoE damage. ==Combat stats== {{Infobox Bonuses |bucketname = Active |astab = +85 |aslash = +110 |acrush = +5 |amagic = -4 |arange = 0 |dstab = -1 |dslash = +4 |dcrush = +5 |dmagic = 0 |drange = 0 |str = +118 |rstr = 0 |mdmg = 0 |prayer = 0 |slot = 2h |speed = 7 |attackrange = 2 |combatstyle = Polearm |image = [[File:Crystal halberd equipped male.png|170px]] |altimage = [[File:Crystal halberd equipped female.png|170px]] }} {{CombatStyles|Polearm|speed=7|attackrange=2}} ==Special attack== [[File:Sweep (crystal halberd).gif|thumb|250px|A player performing the crystal halberd's special attack, ''Sweep''.]] [[File:Sweep (crystal halberd, PvP).gif|thumb|250px|A player performing the crystal halberd's special attack in PvP.]] The crystal halberd shares the same [[special attacks|special attack]] as the [[dragon halberd]], consuming 30% of the player's special attack energy. In addition to a 10% damage boost, if used against "large" monsters (anything that is larger than 1x1, such as [[General Graardor]]), the special attack will deal an additional second hit onto them, although with 25% reduced accuracy. It can also hit up to 10 NPCs in a [[multi-combat area]] in a 3x1 area centred on the main target (unless it is used on an NPC larger than 1x1, in which case it will hit the target twice and ignore the rest of the NPCs). In [[PvP]], it can only hit up to three players. The crystal halberd's special attack's accuracy rolls against the opponent's slash defence.{{CiteTwitter|author=Mod Ash|url=https://twitter.com/JagexAsh/status/690476787806158848|date=22 January 2016|archiveurl=https://reldo.runescape.wiki/i/100957|archivedate=28 May 2020}} When using gear with the highest strength bonus and a [[Slayer helmet]] or [[black mask]] on task, the highest possible hit with the special attack is 74–74 (total of 148) on large monsters. While this is higher than what [[dragon claws]] and [[dragon dagger]] can achieve, the much slower attack speed of the halberd is a major downside, so the claws and dagger generally lead to faster damage output in combat. The crystal halberd's special attack can still be particularly useful in certain situations. Anywhere that players have a forced amount of downtime (time where it is impossible to continue attacking), a crystal halberd special attack can be substituted for extra damage without incurring the 7-tick penalty. This is a common tactic against the [[Pestilent Bloat]]; due to his long period of time where you have to run away, players typically use a crystal halberd special attack as their last damaging attack before running away to increase their potential damage cap. This tactic is also commonly utilized when fighting [[Zulrah]] with a melee setup; the crystal halberd special attack is used right before Zulrah dives into the swamp, with the damage registering after she resurfaces. Because she is temporarily untargetable after diving, the special attack's downside of a 7-tick attack penalty is made irrelevant, allowing its high damage output to be more beneficial. In a similar manner, it is often used when the opponent is on low health and the special attack is likely to finish them faster than using standard weapons (a strategy known as "last-hitting"). Although halberds are the only weapon category alongside [[spear]]s to ignore the damage reduction on the [[Corporeal Beast]], the crystal halberd has limited use on it; as the special attack rolls against the opponent's slash defence, which is particularly high on the Corporeal Beast, it is very inaccurate against it (typically to the point where [[dragon claws]] are more effective despite the damage reduction) unless its Defence level is severely reduced. The special attack's ability to hit up to ten enemies can prove useful as well; when on a [[Slayer]] task where stacking enemies up in one pile to cast multi-combat spells is efficient, such as [[smoke devil]]s, the crystal halberd can deal a large amount of additional damage, often twice that of an [[Ice Barrage]]. It is often used for this purpose in efficient [[Barbarian Assault]] teams by the attacker. ==Creation== {{#vardefine:crystalshardvalue|{{#ifexpr:{{#vardefineecho:seedvalue|{{GEP|Enhanced crystal teleport seed}} / 150}} > {{#vardefineecho:divinesupercombatvalue|({{GEP|Divine super combat potion(4)}} - {{GEP|Super combat potion(4)}}) / 0.4}} | {{#var:seedvalue}} | {{#var:divinesupercombatvalue}}}} round 1}} |-| Crafting = {{Recipe |skill1 = Smithing |skill1lvl = 78 |skill1boostable = Yes |skill1exp = 2,000 |skill2 = Crafting |skill2lvl = 78 |skill2boostable = Yes |skill2exp = 2,000 |notes = {{SCP|Quest}} Completion of [[Song of the Elves]]
{{SCP|Diary}} Completion of [[Hard Western Provinces Diary]] |members = Yes |ticks = 3 |ticksnote = Takes 3 ticks to create an entire inventory |facilities = Singing bowl |mat1 = Crystal weapon seed |mat2 = Crystal shard |mat2quantity = 40 |mat2cost = {{#var:crystalshardvalue}} |mat2itemnote = Value based on {{#ifexpr:{{#var:seedvalue}} > {{#var:divinesupercombatvalue}} | the cost of obtaining by exchanging [[enhanced crystal teleport seed]]s with [[Amrod]] | the potential profit of using the crystal shards to create [[divine super combat potion]]s}} |output1 = Crystal halberd |output1cost = No |output1subtxt = Crafting }} |-| Islwyn = {{Recipe |notes = {{SCP|Quest}} Completion of [[Roving Elves]]
{{SCP|Diary}} Completion of [[Hard Western Provinces Diary]] |members = Yes |ticks = 0 |facilities = Islwyn |mat1 = Coins |mat1quantity = 750000 |mat1cost = 1 |output1 = Crystal halberd |output1cost = No |output1subtxt = Islwyn }} |-| Ilfeen = {{Recipe |notes = {{SCP|Quest}} Completion of [[ Roving Elves]]
{{SCP|Diary}} Completion of [[Hard Western Provinces Diary]] |members = Yes |ticks = 0 |facilities = Ilfeen |mat1 = Crystal weapon seed |mat2 = Coins |mat2quantity = 150000 |mat2quantitynote = Cost from fifth re-enchantment onwards. Initial enchantment is {{NoCoins|750,000|c}}, decreasing with each re-enchantment. |mat2cost = 1 |output1 = Crystal halberd |output1cost = No |output1subtxt = Ilfeen }} ==Degradation== The crystal halberd degrades over time until it reverts into an inactive form. The player must either recharge it with [[crystal shard]]s (if they have completed [[Song of the Elves]]) or revert it into a [[crystal weapon seed]] and re-enchant it via [[Ilfeen]]. [[Ilfeen]] will recharge [[crystal weapon seed]]s into [[crystal bow]]s, [[crystal shield]]s or crystal halberds. She can be found teleporting back and forth between two clearings in [[Isafdar]], or after completion of the [[Song of the Elves]] quest, she can be found south of the [[Prifddinas]] house portal. Initially, Ilfeen charges 750,000 coins to re-enchant a crystal seed into a crystal halberd. However, the price is decreased by 150,000 coins each time Ilfeen re-enchants a seed for a player, in the following manner, until reaching the minimum recharge cost of 150,000 coins: {| class="wikitable" !Enchantment !Cost for re-enchantment !Cost per charge |- |First |{{Coins|750000}} |{{Coins|300}} |- |Second |{{Coins|600000}} |{{Coins|240}} |- |Third |{{Coins|450000}} |{{Coins|180}} |- |Fourth |{{Coins|300000}} |{{Coins|120}} |- |Fifth and thereafter |{{Coins|150000}} |{{Coins|60}} |} The amount that Ilfeen charges is dependent on the number of crystal seeds she has re-enchanted, '''not''' the number of crystal halberds she has recharged. For example, a player who pays Ilfeen 900,000 coins to re-enchant a crystal seed into a crystal bow will only have to pay her 600,000 coins to recharge a seed into a crystal halberd. For players who have completed [[Song of the Elves]], an alternative way of making crystal weapons is using a crystal weapon seed and 40 [[crystal shard]]s along with 78 [[Smithing]] and 78 [[Crafting]] on a [[singing bowl]] in [[Prifddinas]]. Players who do not meet the skill requirements can pay a nearby elf to create the weapon for an additional 20 shards. Crystal shards can be used to recharge the weapon to a maximum of 20,000 charges, with each shard providing 100 charges. However, it is far more cost efficient in the long term to instead use the [[Crystal shard#Value|crystal shards on potions]] and use the money from selling them to re-enchant your crystal equipment. ==Shop locations== {{Store locations list|Crystal halberd}} ==Used in recommended equipment== {{Used in recommended equipment|Crystal halberd}} ==Changes== {{Subject changes header}} {{Subject changes |date = 12 March 2025 |update = Permanent Deadman: World 345 Improvements |change = The crystal halberd, [[crystal bow]], and [[crystal shield]] supports a bank item option for changing charges, with all three items sharing the same "Quantity" value, using crystal charges instead of coins. }} {{Subject changes |date = 25 July 2019 |update = Song of the Elves |poll = Song of the Elves rewards |change = Crystal equipment stats no longer become gradually lower as the weapon degrades, converting all imbued crystal halberds into crystal weapon seeds and removing the ability to imbue them through [[Nightmare Zone]]. The crystal halberd can now alternatively be made and charged through crystal shards instead of paying Ilfeen. }} {{Subject changes |date = 23 May 2019 |update = Hosidius Rework and Deadman Beta |change = The offensive and defensive stats of the unimbued crystal halberd now correctly lower as the weapon degrades. }} {{Subject changes |date = 26 April 2019 |type = hotfix |change = The crystal halberd special attack was limited to three targets in PvP, to prevent unintentionally high [[experience]] rates.{{CiteTwitter|author=Old School RuneScape|url=https://twitter.com/OldSchoolRS/status/1121800161104019457|date=26 April 2019|archiveurl=https://reldo.runescape.wiki/i/101623|archivedate=28 May 2020|quote=A hotfix has been deployed to cap the Crystal halberd, Crystal halberd (i), Dragon halberd, and Dragon two handed sword special attacks to hit a maximum of 3 players in PvP. This is to prevent players achieving unintentionally high xp rates while a long term fix is discussed.}} }} {{Subject changes |date = 7 June 2018 |update = The Theatre of Blood |change = The crystal halberd's special attack used to only hit the main target on the central tile, but hit an unlimited amount of monsters on the two adjacent tiles. Now, it caps the maximum number of monsters to 10, but allows monsters standing on the same tile as the primary target to also be affected by the attack. }} {{Subject changes |date = 19 May 2016 |update = Sand Crabs & Soul Bearers |change = The "uncharge" option on the crystal halberd now properly removes the weapon's imbue. Previously, it would result in the message "Nothing interesting happens". }} {{Subject changes |date = 25 June 2015 |update = Boss Room Counters & Platinum Tokens |change = The crystal halberd now becomes cheaper when re-enchanted. }} {{Subject changes |date = 23 April 2015 |update = Crystal Halberd Special & More |poll = Old School Content Poll 32: Resizable Mode |change = A special attack was added. }} {{Subject changes |date = 26 March 2015 |update = System Update (26 March 2015) |change = The New crystal halberd full (i) now functions as a [[halberd]]. }} {{Subject changes |date = 12 March 2015 |update = Diary Skillcape Emote |change = The combat interface no longer shows unarmed option for the crystal halberd, and attacking an opponent no longer shows the message "Unrecognised ranged weapon". }} {{Subject changes footer}} ==Trivia== [[File:2014 Birthday event - Crystal halberd rack.png|thumb|A rack of crystal halberds found in ''Old School RuneScape's'' first birthday party.]] *It is one of the few pieces of equipment that requires three different skill levels to wield ([[Agility]], [[Attack]], [[Strength]]), as well as having several skill requirements, including [[Combat level|level 100 combat]], to obtain from the [[Hard Western Provinces Diary]]. *The crystal halberd replaced a previous item, also named [[Crystal halberd (historical)|Crystal halberd]]. ==References== {{Reflist}} {{Slash weapons}} {{Crystal items}} {{Nightmare Zone}} {{2014 Birthday event}} [[Category:Weapons with Special attacks]] [[Category:Items with charges]]
